    Home › Manufacturers › Vickers

    Vickers

    United Kingdom · Founded 1911

    The VC10 — elegant, over-engineered, and still holding a record.

    Aircraft here1
    Longest52.32 m
    Combined built54
    First flights1962–1962
    The company

    The story so far

    Vickers-Armstrongs built the Viscount, the world's first turboprop airliner, and then the VC10: four engines on the tail, a huge clean wing, and short-field performance designed for hot, high runways that American jets could not use.

    Only 54 were built and it never made money, but it still holds the record for the fastest subsonic crossing of the Atlantic by a jet airliner — five hours and one minute, set in 1979 and never beaten. Vickers became part of the British Aircraft Corporation in 1960.

    The line-up

    Vickers aircraft

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
